commit.message: Fix ClickHouse dependency compatibility

chore(deps): bump the go_modules group across 1 directory with 2 updates

Bumps the go_modules group with 2 updates in the / directory: [github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk) and [github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go).


Updates `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` from 1.1.0 to 1.3.1
- [Release notes](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/compare/v1.1.0...v1.3.1)

Updates `github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go` from 0.58.2 to 0.65.0
- [Release notes](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go/compare/v0.58.2...v0.65.0)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk
  dependency-version: 1.3.1
  dependency-type: direct:production
  dependency-group: go_modules
- dependency-name: github.com/ClickHouse/ch-go
  dependency-version: 0.65.0
  dependency-type: indirect
  dependency-group: go_modules
...

commit.message:
Refactor skills loading and management; remove unused code and tests

- Removed the Loader struct and its Load method, simplifying the skills loading process.
- Deleted the Manager struct and its associated methods, as they were no longer needed.
- Cleaned up the specialists package by removing unused functions and tests related to tool sinks and regex matching.
- Removed deprecated session ID validation functions and their tests.
- Eliminated unnecessary workflow saving functions and tests in the warpp package.
- Added new tests for routing functionality in the specialists package.
refactor: clean up code and improve skills cache invalidation logic

Implement pulse task management and messaging tools

commit.message:
Implement pulse task management and messaging tools

- Add `pulse_store_test.go` to test the functionality of the pulse store.
- Create `service.go` to handle pulse task evaluation and prompt building.
- Implement tests for the pulse service in `service_test.go`.
- Introduce `matrix_room_message` tool for sending messages to Matrix rooms.
- Add tests for the `matrix_room_message` tool in `tool_test.go`.
- Develop `pulse_tasks` tool for managing recurring tasks in Matrix rooms.
- Implement tests for the `pulse_tasks` tool in `tool_test.go`.
- Add `manibot` file for bot configuration.

commit.message:
feat(manibot): add Matrix bot CLI with environment configuration and Docker support
feat(projects): implement directory copy utility and corresponding tests
feat(agent): ensure latest user message is retained during summarization
feat(google): add support for WEBP image format and enhance error handling in ChatWithImageAttachments
feat(google): add support for vision in google provider
feat(vision): enhance vision client handling with support for Anthropic provider
feat(llmparallel): implement parallel completions tool with aggregation and fallback mechanisms
feat: enhance handling of extra parameters and local directory paths in various components

Add tests for bounded responses and API key handling in specialists store

- Implement tests for truncating oversized tool outputs in responses.
- Ensure empty tool outputs are represented as JSON objects.
- Validate that responses are truncated to a custom character limit.
- Sanitize extra fields by removing context management keys.
- Preserve system messages when trimming non-system messages.
- Modify specialists store to retain existing API keys when a blank key is provided.
- Add tests for both preserving and replacing API keys in the specialists store.
feat(ChatView): enhance chat markdown styling for better code block display
feat(ChatView, chatMentions): implement specialist mention resolution and routing
feat(archive): enhance project archive functionality to support subpath downloads

feat: add OpenAPI documentation and handlers

- Implemented OpenAPI specification generation with `GenerateSpecJSON` function.
- Created HTTP handlers for serving OpenAPI JSON and interactive Swagger UI.
- Added HTML template for Swagger UI with functionality to load OpenAPI spec.
- Introduced tests for OpenAPI handlers and specification generation.
- Enhanced API documentation with detailed route specifications and security definitions.
feat(ChatView, ProjectsView): implement delete confirmation dialogs for sessions and projects

feat(flow): implement workflow validation and compilation

- Added `ValidateWorkflow` function to validate workflow definitions and return diagnostics for errors and warnings.
- Introduced `CompileWorkflow` function to compile validated workflows into execution plans.
- Created new types for diagnostics, plans, and workflow structures in `compiler.go`, `contracts.go`, and `types.go`.
- Implemented tests for validation and compilation in `compiler_test.go`.
- Added utility functions for handling extra parameters in LLM tools in `extra_params.go`.
- Developed `AgentResponseTool` for rendering responses in various formats in `agent_response.go` with corresponding tests.
fix(ChatView): adjust class for message count display to prevent text wrapping
feat: update Dockerfiles to include docker.io installation and modify docker-compose for volume mapping
feat: add curl installation to runtime image and update docker-compose for local tmp directory
feat(ChatView): add export conversation button and functionality

feat: add team management functionality and delegate tasks to teams

- Introduced new handlers for managing specialist teams, including listing, creating, updating, and deleting teams.
- Implemented in-memory and PostgreSQL-backed stores for specialist teams.
- Added functionality to delegate tasks to a team's orchestrator via a new DelegateToTeamTool.
- Enhanced context management to forward authentication cookies and session/project IDs during internal service calls.
- Updated UI components to filter and display specialists, excluding team-specific orchestrators.
- Added tests for the new delegation tool and team management features.

refactor: rename specialist groups to teams and update related components

- Updated UI components to reflect the change from "groups" to "teams" in SpecialistsView.vue.
- Created EditTeamRoot.vue for editing team details, including orchestrator configuration and member management.
- Added migration script to rename database tables and backfill data from groups to teams.
- Adjusted API calls and data handling to support the new team structure.
chore: remove .gitmodules file
chore: remove external whisper subproject
style(ChatView): adjust margin for project and render mode labels
style(ChatView): improve layout and spacing for project and render mode selectors

Refactor Dockerfile and update STT integration

- Simplified the Dockerfile for building agentd with CUDA support, removing unnecessary comments and steps related to whisper.cpp.
- Updated deployment documentation to include configuration for speech-to-text (STT) using OpenAI API-compatible endpoints.
- Enhanced user guide to clarify STT requirements and troubleshooting steps.
- Added STT configuration options to example.env and the configuration loader.
- Removed whisper.cpp dependencies and related code from the project, streamlining the build process.
- Implemented STT functionality in the audio handler, allowing for audio transcription via a specified API.
- Cleaned up unused code and functions across various files, improving overall code quality and maintainability.
feat(auth): add DisablePKCE option for OAuth2 configuration and update login flow
feat: add thought summary handling and update related components
feat: implement OAuth token refresh and reauthentication for MCP servers

feat: add specialist groups and memberships functionality

- Implemented database migrations for specialist_groups and specialist_group_memberships tables.
- Created a new handler for managing groups, including listing, creating, updating, and deleting groups.
- Developed a persistence layer for specialist groups with both in-memory and PostgreSQL implementations.
- Added a Vue component for editing group details, orchestrator settings, and managing group members.
- Introduced functionality for managing group memberships, including adding and removing specialists from groups.

feat(logging): add log metrics handler and integrate with ClickHouse

- Implemented a new log metrics handler in `handlers_tools.go` to fetch logs from ClickHouse.
- Added a new route for log metrics in `router.go`.
- Introduced `clickhouseLogMetrics` to handle log queries in `logs_clickhouse.go`.
- Created a new `OTelWriter` to bridge zerolog output to OpenTelemetry logs in `otelwriter.go`.
- Updated observability initialization to include OTLP log exporter in `otel.go`.
- Added a new composable `useLogMetrics` for fetching and managing log data in the UI.
- Enhanced the UI with a new `LogsPanel` component to display logs with filtering options.
- Updated the OverviewView to include the new LogsPanel.
- Modified the client API to support fetching log metrics.

Add parallel LLM agent architecture report, RAG example workflow, and new utility functions

- Created a new report on efficient parallel architectures and methodologies for LLM agents, detailing their foundations, architectural patterns, execution strategies, and future directions.
- Added a JSON example for a RAG (Retrieve and Generate) workflow that fetches a URL, ingests it into a database, and retrieves document chunks based on a query.
- Implemented a helper function in Go for generating snippet previews from content.
- Introduced new SVG icon components for SolarCopy, SolarRefresh, and SolarTrash in the agent UI.
fix vision support on all providers
Convert imggen.png to WebP
Add per-session chat projects and workflow project assignment
Scope specialist selection per session
Update project handling and chat UI
Switch skills folder to .skills

Refactor encryption handling in project service

- Removed legacy master key handling and associated methods.
- Simplified encryption logic by relying solely on KeyProvider for DEK management.
- Updated project creation, file upload, and reading methods to reflect changes in encryption strategy.
- Deleted related test file for service encryption as functionality has been consolidated.
Remove enterprise-only functionality

feat: update codebase for enterprise mode support

- Added build constraints for enterprise mode in various files including config tests, object store, orchestrator, skills, and workspaces.
- Refactored MCPServerPool to clarify per-user instance requirements and adjusted related tests.
- Simplified CacheService by removing Redis and S3 dependencies, focusing on local filesystem caching.
- Updated Kafka tool documentation to reflect its unavailability in local-only deployments.
- Removed unused code and tests related to ephemeral workspace management, ensuring legacy workspace management is the only supported mode.
feat: replace mergeAnyMap with copyAnyMap for ExtraParams handling and improve workspace manager commit logic
feat(tests): enhance test setup and add mocks for components and API interactions

Refactor and enhance error handling across various components

- Added error handling for `filepath.Rel` in `migrateprojects` and `migrateprojects-s3` commands.
- Improved error checking for `head` operations in `migrateprojects-s3`.
- Updated Kafka broker parsing in orchestrator integration tests and tools to handle CSV input.
- Enhanced logging and error reporting in orchestrator main and tools.
- Introduced a new utility function for parsing comma-separated values.
- Refactored the `whisper-go` main function to improve file existence checks and error handling.
- Adjusted observability logging to provide clearer error messages.
- Implemented a new function to validate project and session IDs, improving code reuse.
- Added unit tests for project and session ID validation functions.
- Updated versioning comments for clarity.
feat(encryption): implement file encryption for project files and enhance workspace managers with encryption support
feat(filetool): add delete tool for file and directory removal with recursive support
feat(llm): add support for extra parameters in LLM configurations and requests
feat(streaming): enhance SSE handling with mutex for concurrent writes and keep-alive support
feat(logging): enhance error logging for agent run and prompt handlers; add context for stream aborts

commit.message: feat(ui): stream thought summaries in Active Specialist panel
feat: add back-compat handling for top-level summary parameter in reasoning summary extraction
feat: implement dual summary handling and prompt caching for Anthropic integration
feat: enable message summarization and enhance engine configuration options
Add multitool parallel runner and update scripts

Refactor Google LLM Client Tests and Remove Parallel Tool Implementation

- Added support for thought summaries in the streamRecorder struct and related test cases.
- Implemented tests to verify inclusion of thought summaries based on model names.
- Enhanced the ChatStream functionality to handle thought summaries and incremental thoughts.
- Removed the entire multitool parallel implementation, including its tests and related structures.
- Deleted subtool sink functionality as it was no longer needed.
feat: add OnThoughtSummary handler and UI support for reasoning summaries
feat: enhance tool call handling and response processing for improved streaming support

feat: add file handling tools for reading, writing, and patching files

- Implemented `file_read` tool to read single or multiple files with size limits.
- Implemented `file_write` tool to create or overwrite files with specified content and encoding.
- Implemented `file_patch` tool to replace specific line ranges in files without modifying other lines.
- Enhanced the `Engine` to ensure unique tool call IDs during execution.
- Added observability logging for Google and Anthropic LLM clients, including duration metrics.
- Updated UI to support mention functionality for specialists in chat input.

feat: Enhance compaction logic and introduce continuation rule

- Added compaction best-practice knobs to manage message compaction more effectively.
- Introduced a continuation rule to avoid restating prior final answers unless requested.
- Updated the BuildContext method to prepend the continuation rule in compaction mode.
- Modified the ensureSummary method to handle compaction conditions based on message deltas and tool outputs.
- Implemented a new estimateMessagesTokens function to better estimate token usage for messages.
- Enhanced the compactChunk method to ensure compaction requests fit within model context limits and to truncate message contents as needed.
- Updated tests to reflect changes in expected message counts and summarized counts after compaction.
feat: add participants sidebar with active specialist display and participant status
feat: add response status display in chat view for better user feedback
feat: implement user-specific delegator in cloneEngineForUser for enhanced tool management
fix: update tool registry references to baseToolRegistry and adjust UI layout spacing
feat: enhance evolving memory management with session support and improve API interactions

feat: enhance evolving memory management and summary configuration

- Introduced user-scoped evolving memory to avoid shared system-level memory when auth is enabled.
- Added configuration options for summary context window size and maximum messages to keep in raw form.
- Implemented filtering of orphan tool outputs in the Responses API to prevent errors.
- Updated environment variable loading to support new summary configuration options.
- Enhanced tests to validate the new behavior of input handling for tool outputs.
Fix admin role check in memory handler and token metrics interface
Scope Overview token/traces metrics per user via enduser.id
Scope Overview observability endpoints by user (admin-only) and tag LLM spans
Scope orchestrator system prompt specialists per user
Fix specialists catalog leakage across users
Improve frontend code quality and fix per user orchestrator config

feat: add user preferences management for active project

- Implemented UserPreferencesStore to persist user-specific settings.
- Integrated user preferences into the MCP server pool for per-user session management.
- Enhanced workspace checkout process to notify MCP pool of active project changes.
- Updated API client to handle user preferences retrieval and updates.
- Modified projects store to initialize from user preferences on app mount.
- Added tests for user preferences store functionality.
fix(README): remove redundant contributor information for clarity
Update UI examples in README

feat(workspaces): implement enterprise workspace manager with Redis and Kafka integration

- Added EnterpriseWorkspaceManager to handle workspace operations with caching and event publishing.
- Integrated Redis for generation caching and invalidation notifications.
- Implemented Kafka for publishing project commit events.
- Introduced TmpfsMaterializer for managing temporary file systems.
- Created RedisGenerationCache for managing project generation state via Redis.
- Developed KafkaCommitPublisher for emitting commit events to Kafka.
- Added integration tests to verify workspace lifecycle and concurrent operations.
- Ensured backward compatibility with legacy workspace mode.
- Implemented validation for project and session IDs to prevent path traversal attacks.

## What's Changed
* refactor: clean up code and improve skills cache invalidation logic by @Art9681 in https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/pull/146
* chore(deps): bump the go_modules group across 1 directory with 2 updates by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/pull/142

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/compare/2026.03.2...2026.03.3

## What's Changed
* Multi-provider vision support, better memory, and chatbot over Matrix backend by @Art9681 in https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/pull/144
* Implement pulse task management and messaging tools by @Art9681 in https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/pull/145


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/intelligencedev/manifold/compare/2026.03.1...2026.03.2

readme_html:
manifold

Manifold is an experimental platform for enabling long horizon workflow automation using teams of AI assistants.

Manifold supports OpenAI models such as gpt-5.2, Google models such as gemini-3-pro and Anthropic models such as claude-opus-4-5. Manifold also supports OpenAI API compatible services for users that self host open weight models such as gpt-oss-120b, devstral-2-123b using llama.cpp or vllm.

Disclaimer: As an experimental frontier AI platform, we do not recommend Manifold be deployed in a production environment where stability is required until explicitly noted in this README.

Features

Agent Chat

Use a traditional chat view to instruct specialists (agents) to work on objectives.

Specialists can collaborate in multi-turn objectives. Manifold is designed to take advantage of the long horizon capabilities of frontier models and can work on complex objectives for hours.

Image Generation

Manifold supports OpenAI and Google image generation models as well as local image generation using a custom ComfyUI MCP client.

Example ComfyUI generated image with a custom workflow

Specialist Registry

Define and configure AI agents (specialists) and build your team of experts.

Projects

Configure projects as agent workspaces.

Integrated tools and MCP Support

Manifold implements internal tools for agent workflows as well as MCP support to extend the capabilities of your agents. Configure as many MCP servers as you wish. Enable tools individually to easily manage model context limits.

Workflow Editor

Design agent workflows using a visual flow editor.

Prompts, Datasets and Experiments Playground

Create, iterate and version custom prompts that can be assigned to your agents. Configure datasets and run experiments to understand how prompts affect agent behaviors.

Quick Start

For step-by-step quick start instructions, see the repository Quick Start guide: QUICKSTART.md

API Docs

OpenAPI generation and API docs publishing workflow is documented in docs/openapi.md.

Matrix bot CLI (manibot)

Manifold includes a Matrix bot CLI that forwards room messages to the Manifold backend (/api/prompt), so responses can use your configured specialists, internal tools, MCP servers, and project skills.

Detailed setup, .env template, and a minimal Docker Compose service example are in cmd/manibot/README.md.

Run:

go run ./cmd/manibot

Required environment variables:

  • MATRIX_HOMESERVER_URL
  • MATRIX_BOT_USER_ID
  • MATRIX_ACCESS_TOKEN

Optional variables:

  • BOT_PREFIX (default: !bot)
  • MANIFOLD_BASE_URL (default: http://localhost:32180)
  • MANIFOLD_PROMPT_PATH (default: /api/prompt)
  • MANIFOLD_PROJECT_ID (bind all prompts to one project/workspace)
  • MANIFOLD_SESSION_PREFIX (default: matrix)
  • MANIFOLD_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default: 180)
  • MATRIX_SYNC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default: 30)
  • MATRIX_SYNC_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS (default: 3)
  • MATRIX_PULSE_ENABLED (default: false; enables the background pulse loop)
  • MATRIX_PULSE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS (default: 300; how often the bot checks all room task lists)
  • MATRIX_PULSE_LEASE_SECONDS (default: MANIFOLD_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS + 60; claim window to prevent duplicate pulse runs)
  • PULSE_DATABASE_DSN (required when pulse is enabled unless one of DATABASE_URL, DB_URL, or POSTGRES_DSN is already set)
  • MANIFOLD_SESSION_COOKIE and MANIFOLD_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME (for auth-enabled agentd cookie auth)
  • MANIFOLD_AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN (optional bearer header passthrough)

Matrix Pulse Automation

manibot can run a background pulse loop that checks room-scoped recurring tasks and submits due work to the Manifold orchestrator as an automated prompt.

Operator model:

  • Pulse is room-scoped. Each Matrix room has its own task list.
  • Pulse runs use a separate session from normal room chat, so automated context does not pollute human chat history.
  • Due-task state is persisted in the shared database and claimed with a lease so multiple bot instances do not run the same room pulse simultaneously.
  • The agent can manage tasks through the pulse_tasks tool during both manual chat runs and automated pulse runs.

Recommended setup:

  • Enable pulse only when manibot can reach the same Postgres database used by Manifold persistence.
  • Set PULSE_DATABASE_DSN explicitly in deployments where the bot process does not already inherit DATABASE_URL.
  • Keep MATRIX_PULSE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS shorter than the longest task cadence you care about; per-task intervals are enforced independently of the poll interval.

The pulse_tasks tool currently supports list, configure_room, upsert_task, delete_task, enable_task, disable_task, and set_interval.
